finding.contract_claim: The skill instructs users to 'Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py' when investigating false positives. While tool schemas are indeed in tools.py, the system prompts are located in the individual module files, not in tools.py. This misdirects investigators to the wrong file for critical context. [evidence: src\osoji\deadcode.py — "_DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (L373–428) encodes the full decision logic for the LLM"]
finding.observed_behavior: Change the instruction to: 'Read the tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py and the system prompts in the specific module that generates the finding (e.g., _DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in deadcode.py, _ANALYZE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in doc_analysis.py, system prompts in junk_cicd.py, triage.py, plumbing.py, etc.)'

payload.snippet:
1: ---
2: name: osoji-triage
3: description: Triage findings and produce a structured report (read-only, no fixes)
4: arguments: Optional category or path filter
5: ---
6: 
7: You are reviewing the audit findings from an osoji audit of the CURRENT working
8: directory project. Your goal is to classify each finding as a true positive, false
9: positive, or downgrade to info, then propose improvements for both this repo and
10: osoji itself.
11:

references.context:
"symbol": null,
        "contract_source": "audit",
        "contract_claim": "The skill instructs users to 'Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py' when investigating false positives. While tool schemas are indeed in tools.py, the system prompts are located in the individual module files, not in tools.py. This misdirects investigators to the wrong file for critical context. [evidence: src\\osoji\\deadcode.py — \"_DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (L373–428) encodes the full decision logic for the LLM\"]",
        "observed_behavior": "Change the instruction to: 'Read the tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py and the system prompts in the specific module that generates the finding (e.g., _DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in deadcode.py, _ANALYZE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in doc_analysis.py, system prompts in junk_cicd.py, triage.py, plumbing.py, etc.)'",
        "evidence": [],
references.needle: Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py

references.context:
"confidence": 0.95,
    "triage_reasoning": "Reality: The bootstrap adjudication notes are explicit: 'SKILL.md directs agents to system prompts in tools.py; zero SYSTEM_PROMPT constants there - all 19 live in detector modules. Misdirects FP root-cause tracing.' The evidence sweep confirmed this via 'grep SYSTEM_PROMPT across src' and 'read tools.py confirming' — tools.py holds tool schema definitions only, while every system prompt lives in the individual detector module (deadcode.py, doc_analysis.py, junk_cicd.py, triage.py, plumbing.py, etc.).

Significance: The osoji-triage SKILL.md is the primary navigation artifact for AI agents investigating false positives. Pointing to the wrong file for system prompts means an agent probing a suspected false positive traces to the wrong location, wastes turns, and may fail to surface the actual LLM decision logic.

Actionability: Update the instruction in SKILL.md to direct users to both tools.py (for tool schemas) and the specific detector module that generated the finding (for system prompts).",
    "suggested_fix": "In .claude/skills/osoji-triage/SKILL.md (and its canonical copy at src/osoji/skills/osoji-triage.md), change the instruction from:
  'Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py'
to:
  'Read the tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py and the system prompts in the specific module that generates the finding (e.g., _DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in deadcode.py, _ANALYZE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in doc_analysis.py, system prompts in junk_cicd.py, triage.py, plumbing.py, etc.)'

Both the .claude/ mirror and src/osoji/skills/ canonical must be updated together (parity enforced by tests/test_skills_parity.py).",
    "severity": "warning",
    "evidence_fingerprint": "21b40fbdceec41ba"
references.needle: Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py

needles: Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py
_DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (L373–428) encodes the full decision logic for the LLM

finding.triage_reasoning:
Reality: The bootstrap adjudication evidence is explicit — 'SKILL.md directs agents to system prompts in tools.py; zero SYSTEM_PROMPT constants there – all 19 live in detector modules.' The shadow doc for src/osoji/deadcode.py confirms `_DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (L373–428)` lives in deadcode.py, not in tools.py. The skill fragment shown (L1-11) doesn't include the offending instruction, but prior triage rounds confirm the phrase 'Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py' is present in the skill and is incorrect.

Significance: The osoji-triage SKILL.md is the primary navigation artifact for AI agents investigating false positives. Pointing to the wrong file for system prompts means an agent probing a suspected FP traces to an empty location and cannot inspect the actual LLM decision logic that drove the finding.

Actionability: The fix is concrete: update the instruction in SKILL.md to direct users to tools.py for tool schemas, and to the specific detector module for system prompts (deadcode.py, doc_analysis.py, junk_cicd.py, triage.py, plumbing.py, etc.).
finding.suggested_fix: In .claude/skills/osoji-triage/SKILL.md (and its canonical copy at src/osoji/skills/osoji-triage.md if it exists), change the instruction from 'Read the system prompts and tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py' to 'Read the tool schemas in src/osoji/tools.py and the system prompts in the specific module that generates the finding (e.g., _DEAD_CODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in deadcode.py, _ANALYZE_SYSTEM_PROMPT in doc_analysis.py, system prompts in junk_cicd.py, triage.py, plumbing.py, etc.)'.
